title: angelica gigas
intro: Angelica gigas, also known as Korean angelica or dong quai's cousin, is a species of flowering plant in the carrot family, native to East Asia. The roots and rhizomes of this plant have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral effects. The health benefits of Angelica gigas include its ability to aid in digestion, relieve menstrual cramps, and lower blood pressure. Angelica gigas can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or dried root, and is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
